name: Watercress & Salmon Roulade with Orange Salad
recipeInstructions:
1. Pre-heat oven to 200°C, 400°F, Gas mark 6.
2. Drain the can of salmon and reserve the juice. Flake the fish into a bowl.
3. Line a 35cm x 14inch x 20cm x 8inch swiss roll tin with silicone paper and brush lightly with oil.
4. Add one bunch of watercress to a bowl. Cut two oranges into segments and squeeze the juice from the third orange. Mix the orange segments, carrot pieces, and watercress together and set aside.
5. In a small pan, melt margarine and stir in flour. Gradually add salmon stock, stirring to avoid lumps. Add prawns and mushrooms, cooking gently for two minutes before incorporating the flaked salmon and mixing well. Set aside.
6. Chop the remaining two bunches of watercress finely. Beat egg yolks with chopped watercress and season.
7. Whisk egg whites until forming soft peaks, then fold into the watercress mixture.
8. Spread the batter evenly in the lined tin and bake for 10 minutes.
9. Remove from oven, loosen the sides of the roulade, turn out onto a flat surface, peel away silicone paper, spread salmon sauce over the roulade, roll up in a swiss roll fashion, and trim edges.
10. Serve with slices of the roulade and orange juice dressing over watercress salad. Serves 6. Approx. 215 kcals per serving
